channel.title: Vynix Blog
channel.description: Practical guides on bug reporting, developer context, and shipping with AI coding agents.

item.title: What a feedback layer is, and why AI teams need one
item.description: A practical guide to feedback layers, why AI coding agents need better context, and how Vynix turns website issues into build-ready tasks.

item.title: Turn a visual note into a GitHub issue in one step
item.description: Use Vynix to turn a clicked website problem into a GitHub issue with screenshot, element details, logs, and AI diagnosis.

item.title: How Vynix Captures Console and Network Detail
item.description: See how Vynix collects console logs, network context, screenshots, and element detail so developers can debug reported site issues faster.

item.title: Using the Vynix MCP server with your AI agent
item.description: Connect Vynix context to an AI coding agent so bugs include the element, screenshot, console, network data, and a clearer fix path.

item.title: Collecting website feedback from clients and teammates
item.description: A practical guide to collecting website feedback with enough context for developers to understand, reproduce, and fix issues faster.

item.title: How Vynix Works, From a Click to a Fix
item.description: See how Vynix turns a page click into developer context, an AI root-cause guess, and a ready prompt or GitHub issue.

item.title: Point and click feedback on any web page
item.description: Capture UI bugs with a click, including screenshots, element data, console and network context, and a ready-to-build prompt.

item.title: Review rounds: turn notes into a short fix list
item.description: Learn how to turn messy website review notes into clear, buildable fixes with better triage, context, and Vynix annotations.

item.title: Region and element screenshots in Vynix
item.description: Learn how Vynix uses region and element screenshots to turn visual bug reports into focused developer context, prompts, and GitHub issues.

item.title: Projects, roles and sharing in Vynix
item.description: How to organize Vynix projects, set practical roles, and share annotated website context without losing debugging details.

item.title: How to install Vynix on any website
item.description: Install Vynix with a lightweight widget, verify capture, control where it loads, and turn visual bugs into actionable context.

item.title: How to connect GitHub and hand work to Copilot
item.description: Turn website bugs into GitHub issues Copilot can act on, with screenshots, element context, console logs, and network clues from Vynix.

item.title: Vynix plans, and how to pick the right one
item.description: Compare Vynix plan types at a high level and choose based on bug volume, team workflow, and AI-assisted development needs.

item.title: What we are building next at Vynix
item.description: A practical look at where Vynix is headed: deeper agent workflows, more integrations, and richer context for website feedback.

item.title: The context an AI agent needs to fix a front-end bug
item.description: Learn what to send an AI coding agent for front-end bugs: element details, screenshots, console logs, network traces, and clear repro steps.

item.title: How to Write Bug Reports AI Coding Agents Can Fix
item.description: Learn how to write bug reports with the evidence, context, and task shape an AI coding agent needs to fix the issue.

item.title: From a reported bug to a merged fix
item.description: Turn a vague website bug report into a clear GitHub issue, agent-ready prompt, tested pull request, and merged fix with Vynix.

item.title: Who Vynix Is For and What It Solves
item.description: See who Vynix helps and how it turns vague website feedback into actionable developer context.

item.title: Reading the Vynix AI Diagnosis and Files It Finds
item.description: Learn how to turn a Vynix AI diagnosis, captured context, and suggested files into a clear fix plan or coding-agent prompt.

item.title: How Vynix cuts the cost of AI coding agents
item.description: Vynix gives AI coding agents page, element, console, and network context up front, reducing wasted runs and engineer handoff time.
